user: How do we change that so Intervals has the power? And uses it?
assistant:
Two things need to be set in Intervals.icu (web UI only — no API for sport settings):

*Settings → Sports → Run:*

1. *Running FTP (watts)* — currently null. Intervals can't calculate power-based TSS without a threshold value. Your easy run averaged 416W, so threshold is probably around *340–360W* — but that's a guess. Best to run a 20-min all-out effort and use 95% of average power, or use the Garmin Running Power test protocol.

2. *Power field* — currently null. Garmin stores running power in a developer data field (not the standard cycling power field). Intervals.icu may or may not auto-detect it — worth checking if power shows up once FTP is set.

Once running FTP is set, Intervals will use power for TSS and zone time-in-zone on all future runs (and can recalculate past ones).

Do you want to sort out a threshold running power value first, or go in and set a ballpark figure and refine later?

Those 20-min bests are easy running power — not threshold. They reflect pace around 4:45–4:55/km GAP, which is ~20% slower than your threshold (4:02/km).

*The problem:* running FTP ≈ 95% of best 20-min all-out effort. Your 20-min bests here aren't all-out — they're from Z2 sessions. Setting FTP from them would put your training zones too low.

*Best estimate from pace ratio:* threshold pace is ~20% faster than your current easy pace. Garmin running power scales roughly linearly with speed for a given runner. That puts a rough threshold power at *~495–510W* — but that's an extrapolation, not a measured value.

*Recommendation:* two options —

1. *Set 500W as a temporary placeholder now* so Intervals starts calculating power-based TSS. Refine it properly the first week you can run at threshold pace (ankle permitting). Any error will be systematic and consistent until then.

2. *Leave it blank until you can test properly.* Ankle rehab runs won't give you a real FTP — the first time you run comfortably at 4:02/km effort will.
